name: Lil'Jordan Humphrey
headline: Deep-roster Bronco with a usage ceiling he can't crack
body:
The profile hasn't budged. Across seven games in 2025, Humphrey logged just 25 targets and a 5% target share, turning them into 13 catches for 156 yards and a score on a thin 0.95 yards per route run. His 0.317 EPA per play and 48% success rate read fine in isolation, but at a 26% route rate they're a sample, not a role. Even his 2024 high-water mark topped out at an 8.2% target share, so the ranking sitting at 130 among wideouts reflects the opportunity, not bad luck.

Denver brought him back for a familiar depth job, and the room ahead of him is crowded enough that he projects as a special-teams body more than a target earner. He's off the draft board in every format, with an ADP near pick 570 and essentially zero rostership. File him as an injury-contingency name only.
